title: Eyes
content:
O strange devices that alone divide
 The se�r from the seen - 
 The very highway of earth's pomp and pride
 That lies between
 The traveller and the cheating, sweet delight
 Of where he longs to be,
 But which, bound hand and foot, he, close on night,
 Can only see.
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Eyes"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
